blurb: Paul’s words catch us where grief feels endless: he doesn’t promise instant relief, only that the dead in Christ rise first, so our tears are not the last chapter. Hold that hope like a lantern today—every sigh, every empty chair, is already seen by the One who descends with a shout and turns sorrow into morning.

verses.text: But I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep, that ye sorrow not, even as others which have no hope.
For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so them also which sleep in Jesus will God bring with him.
For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord, that we which are alive and remain unto the coming of the Lord shall not prevent them which are asleep.
For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel, and with the trump of God: and the dead in Christ shall rise first:
